title: stelis caespitula
intro: Stelis caespitula is a species of orchid, commonly known as the "False Yellow Axil Flower," native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and the treatment of various infections. The health benefits of Stelis caespitula include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. The herbal preparation of Stelis caespitula typically involves drying the flowers and leaves, which can then be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
